blocksCertification: Distinct embedded verified products = 5 (Honeycomb Alpha, Honeycomb Bravo, TCA Sidestick, TCA Quadrant, TCA Boeing Yoke). This is below the >=8-verified-product-visuals aspiration, but it is the honest ceiling for a two-ecosystem head-to-head: the comparison's real cast is the GA pair vs the Airbus pair plus the Boeing fork. Total visuals (12: hero + 4-figure shotgrid + 6 gearpicks, several reusing the same verified photos) meet the visual-density bar; distinct products are capped by the topic. Not a defect, but noted honestly against the numeric target.

The already-verified Honeycomb Charlie pedals photo is referenced text/link-only (natural-next GA piece), not embedded. Promoting it to a 6th gearpick would raise distinct embedded products to 6 if desired before certification.

Prices left as ranges / check-current where sale- or channel-dependent: Alpha + Bravo bundle (~$600 official, no single SKU price on official pages, bundle discounts vary), TCA Quadrant Airbus ($129.99 official MSRP vs ~$100 street), TCA Yoke Pack Boeing ($549.99 official, current street not confirmed). Confirm exact figures before certifying.
TCA Sidestick action-button count: used Thrustmaster's official '17 action buttons'; a third-party guide cited 12 remappable. Reconcile before certifying if exactness is required.

Gear-page links (/flight/gear/honeycomb-charlie-pedals etc.) and /flight/best-flight-sim-hardware-2026 build only when their targets are certified/have verified images; internal links follow the same forward-compatible pattern as the reference gold article. Confirm no dead internal links block the gate before certifying.

Article intentionally does not emit a public page: isCertifiedArticle requires goldStatus==='certified' and this is left as 'refresh' per run rules. npx astro build completed cleanly (166 pages, no errors).
